---
title: "Dashboard"
format:
  dashboard:
    orientation: rows
    theme: [flatly, theme.scss]
    nav-buttons:
      - icon: linkedin
        href: https://www.linkedin.com/search/results/all/?keywords=Thomas%20Prata
logo: images/headshot.jpg
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go

# NOTE: Figures below are illustrative estimates built from publicly
# available company milestones (founding year, current employee count,
# current valuation, and 2023-2025 attendee totals reported on
# preventems.com). They are intended to demonstrate dashboard design and
# data storytelling, not to represent audited financials.

growth = pd.DataFrame({
    "year": [2022, 2023, 2024, 2025],
    "employees": [18, 140, 340, 580],
    "valuation_m": [0.6, 4.2, 9.0, 15.0],
})

attendees = pd.DataFrame({
    "year": [2023, 2024, 2025],
    "attendees_m": [1.7, 2.1, 2.3],
})

event_scale = pd.DataFrame({
    "event_size": ["Under 5,000", "5,000 – 50,000", "50,000 – 185,000+"],
    "share_of_events": [55, 33, 12],
})

market = pd.DataFrame({
    "company": ["TJX / TJ Maxx", "Starbucks", "AlliedBarton"],
    "settlement_m": [8.5, 23, 11],
})
```

# Prevent Medical Solutions

## Row {height="20%"}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Current Employees"
#| icon: people-fill
#| color: primary
dict(value = "580+")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Company Valuation"
#| icon: graph-up-arrow
#| color: primary
dict(value = "$15M")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Attendees Protected (2023–25)"
#| icon: shield-check
#| color: primary
dict(value = "6.1M+")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Largest Single Event"
#| icon: people
#| color: secondary
dict(value = "185,000")
```

## Row {height="80%"}

### Column {width="60%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Headcount & Valuation Growth (2022–2025)"
fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(x=growth["year"], y=growth["employees"], name="Employees",
                      marker_color="#2E6F95", yaxis="y1"))
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=growth["year"], y=growth["valuation_m"], name="Valuation ($M)",
                          mode="lines+markers", marker_color="#0B2545", yaxis="y2"))
fig.update_layout(
    yaxis=dict(title="Employees"),
    yaxis2=dict(title="Valuation ($M)", overlaying="y", side="right"),
    legend=dict(orientation="h", y=1.15),
    margin=dict(t=30, l=10, r=10, b=10),
)
fig.show()
```

### Column {width="40%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Event Scale Mix"
fig2 = px.pie(event_scale, names="event_size", values="share_of_events",
              color_discrete_sequence=["#0B2545", "#2E6F95", "#8DA9C4"], hole=0.45)
fig2.update_layout(margin=dict(t=10, l=10, r=10, b=10), showlegend=True)
fig2.show()
```

```{python}
#| title: "Attendees & Staff Protected Per Year (Millions)"
fig3 = px.bar(attendees, x="year", y="attendees_m", text="attendees_m",
              color_discrete_sequence=["#13315C"])
fig3.update_traces(texttemplate="%{text}M", textposition="outside")
fig3.update_layout(yaxis_title="Attendees (Millions)", xaxis_title="", margin=dict(t=30,l=10,r=10,b=10))
fig3.show()
```

# BreakPlate Market Opportunity

## Row {height="20%"}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Annual CA Wage-and-Hour Liability"
#| icon: exclamation-triangle-fill
#| color: danger
dict(value = "$2B+")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Combined Settlements Shown Below"
#| icon: currency-dollar
#| color: warning
dict(value = "$42.5M")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "PAGA Penalty Reduction with Documented Steps"
#| icon: shield-check
#| color: success
dict(value = "85%")
```

## Row {height="80%"}

### Column {width="55%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Real Cases: Break-Compliance Settlements"
fig4 = px.bar(market, x="company", y="settlement_m", text="settlement_m",
              color_discrete_sequence=["#0B2545"])
fig4.update_traces(texttemplate="$%{text}M", textposition="outside")
fig4.update_layout(yaxis_title="Settlement ($M)", xaxis_title="", margin=dict(t=30,l=10,r=10,b=10))
fig4.show()
```

### Column {width="45%"}

::: {.card-section}
#### Why this matters for BreakPlate's GTM

Every one of these settlements happened because the employer **could not prove** a break was offered — not because breaks weren't taken. That single fact is the entire wedge: existing payroll platforms (Rippling, Gusto, ADP, Paychex) protect employers from the IRS, but none of them generate legally defensible, biometrically-authenticated proof at the moment of clock-out.

This chart is the one slide that reliably gets a head nod from HR and risk-management buyers in early customer conversations — it reframes the product from "another timesheet app" to "the legal defense file your current payroll platform can't produce."
:::

<small>*Data compiled from publicly reported settlement figures and BreakPlate's public marketing materials (breakplate.com), current as of mid-2026.*</small>
